title: epipactis rivularis
intro: Epipactis rivularis, also known as the Marsh Helleborine, is a rare and endangered species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Epipactis rivularis typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and roots, which can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
